concept: Notify / Formally Direct Information to a Recipient
description: For a source to formally direct information toward a recipient. Notifying sends the word; whether it arrived, was understood, or was acted on remains open past the sending itself.
sound_symbolism: The complete verb 'shane' (tell) supplies the directed information, while relational 'wi' sends it toward an identified recipient. The light ending keeps delivery, receipt, accessibility, attention, and understanding as later events.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb with the notifying source as subject, recipient in a dative phrase, and information as object: 'ha punoki wei lo wemari ha kanuro shanewi.' (The institution notifies the members of this decision.) State method, time, format, source, and required response separately. The event-noun rule supplies notification or notice. Distinct from 'shane' (tell), publication, 'sirami' (record), successful receipt, and understanding.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Durable commons need traceable notice about resource changes, hazards, meetings, maintenance, and decisions without assuming one communication channel serves everyone.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating notification from receipt and understanding prevents institutions from blaming excluded people merely because information was sent or posted.

tags.community: formal notice directed to identified recipients
tags.dialogue: information offered without presuming receipt or understanding
